serving.natural_language_portion: One CHICHA San Chen Classic Travel Tumbler contains approximately 500 ml of milk tea served cold with a straw, equivalent to a large single-serve beverage portion.

nutrient_source.title: Milk tea, prepared with whole milk and black tea — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented with NCC Food and Nutrient Database generic milk tea category values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for CHICHA San Chen branded Classic Travel Tumbler. Values estimated from USDA FoodData Central generic milk tea / tea with milk entries (NDB 14355 and related) and cross-referenced with NCC Food and Nutrient Database milk tea category. CHICHA San Chen Classic is a Taiwanese-style milk tea made with brewed black or oolong tea, fresh milk, and sugar. Per-100g values reflect a typical sweetened milk tea composition at approximately 50% tea liquor, 35% milk, and 15% sugar syrup by volume. Serving size set at 500 g (500 ml tumbler). All values are estimates; exact brand formulation is not publicly disclosed.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened milk tea estimated from analogous foods: sweetened tea with milk and sucrose; GI values for milk (GI ~27) and sucrose (GI ~65) blended proportionally per published methodology
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for CHICHA San Chen Classic Milk Tea Travel Tumbler. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for sweetened milk tea and tea with milk and sugar. Whole milk has a GI of approximately 27 (University of Sydney); sucrose GI is 65. A blended sweetened milk tea with approximately 8.8 g sugar per 100 g yields an estimated GI of 55. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 46.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 25.3. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (28 mg per 100 g; approximately 140 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of a single 500 ml serving is approximately 140 mg, which is well within the daily sodium limit of 2000 mg recommended for hypertensive individuals; however, regular consumption of sweetened milk tea should be monitored for overall caloric and sugar load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar content and a low glycemic index (GI ~55) but with a high glycemic load of approximately 25 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: A single 500 ml serving delivers approximately 44 g of sugar and a high glycemic load of 25, which poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit sweetened milk tea and request sugar-free or reduced-sugar options.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from milk and high added sugar contributing to cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 500 ml serving provides approximately 4.5 g of saturated fat and 44 g of added sugar; frequent consumption of sweetened milk tea has been associated with elevated triglycerides and should be limited in individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The CHICHA San Chen Classic Milk Tea Travel Tumbler is a large-format (approximately 500 ml) sweetened milk tea beverage sold by CHICHA San Chen, a Taiwanese-origin specialty tea brand with outlets in Singapore including One Fullerton. The drink is prepared by brewing high-quality black or oolong tea, blending it with fresh milk or creamer, and sweetening with sugar syrup, then sealing it in a travel-friendly tumbler with a straw for takeaway consumption. The flavour profile is smooth, mildly sweet, and creamy with a distinct tea aroma. The texture is liquid with a slightly silky mouthfeel from the milk component. Nutritionally, a single 500 ml serving provides approximately 270 kcal, 46 g of carbohydrate (predominantly from added sugar at approximately 44 g), 7 g of protein, and 7 g of fat. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), but the glycemic load per serving is approximately 25, placing it in the high glycemic load category. This beverage is of significant clinical concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar content and high glycemic load. It is moderately concerning for cardiovascular health due to saturated fat from milk and high sugar intake. Sodium content is low, making it less concerning for hypertension in isolation. The product is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It is not suitable for vegans due to dairy content. CHICHA San Chen is known for using premium tea leaves and fresh milk, distinguishing it from lower-quality bubble tea brands. The travel tumbler format is popular in Singapore as a premium takeaway beverage option.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data exists for CHICHA San Chen Classic Travel Tumbler in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN). Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central generic milk tea and tea-with-milk entries, cross-referenced with NCC Food and Nutrient Database milk tea category. The brand formulation is not publicly disclosed. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and the analogous source is approved, but exact brand-specific values are unavailable.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central generic milk tea / tea with milk entries (NDB 14355 and related) and NCC Food and Nutrient Database milk tea category. The CHICHA San Chen brand does not publicly disclose full nutritional information. 2. Serving size set at 500 g (500 ml) based on standard large travel tumbler format sold at SGD 19.80. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as brewed tea and milk contain negligible dietary fibre; this is naturally zero for a liquid milk tea beverage. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as no partially hydrogenated oils are used in standard milk tea preparation. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; tea and milk contain negligible vitamin C at typical serving concentrations. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central milk tea reference entries and cannot be reliably estimated for this product. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central milk tea reference entries. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central milk tea reference entries. 9. GI estimated at 55 (low) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for sweetened milk tea; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use. 10. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 46.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 25.3. 11. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact branded milk tea tumbler codes exist in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT. 12. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Chinese-cuisine item sold in Singapore. 13. Halal status assumed based on standard CHICHA San Chen Singapore operations; formal halal certification should be verified with the restaurant directly. 14. Price SGD 19.80 preserved from input record.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that dietary_fibre_g and vitamin_c_mg zero values required explicit justification phrases in notes per zero-macro justification rule. Also, gi_category 'medium' was inconsistent with glycemic_index 55 (expected 'low').

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55. Updated gi_source.source_match_notes and health_context.type2_diabetes.key_factor and guidance to reflect the low GI but high GL. Added explicit zero justification phrases for dietary_fibre_g (naturally zero), trans_fat_g (confirmed zero), and vitamin_c_mg (confirmed zero) in notes. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed GI is numeric > 0. Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 now pass.
